title: contingency-dsl Composed — Omission / Negative Automaintenance
description: Composed procedure schema for omission / negative automaintenance (Williams & Williams, 1969). Structurally identical to autoshaping except that any response during the CS cancels the scheduled US on that trial. Diagnostic for the Pavlovian vs operant locus of control of autoshaped responding: persistence of responding under US-cancellation is the empirical signature of Pavlovian control.

OmissionProcedure.description: Pavlovian CS-US pairing with an added response-cancellation rule. The response during CS triggers omission of the programmed US on the same trial.

pairing.description: Respondent primitive (typically Pair.ForwardDelay) that would obtain if no response occurred during CS.

response_class.description: Identifier for the response class that triggers US cancellation. Typically matches the @operandum annotation on the phase (e.g., 'key_peck', 'lever_press'). Cancellation is scoped to this response class only.

cancellation_rule.description: Specifies how a response during CS cancels the US. The DSL does not execute the rule; execution layers consume this declaration.

scope.description: Cancellation scope. Williams & Williams (1969) cancelled the US only for the trial on which the response occurred; subsequent trials proceed normally.
